pattern: Bucketing weights by their value key collapses two lists into one tally, then a single ordered sweep emits the merged result.
hints: You only ever combine entries that share the same value, so group by that value first.
A dictionary keyed on the value lets you accumulate weights from both lists in one pass each.
After summing weights into the map, walk the keys in ascending order to build each [value, totalWeight] row.

edge_inputs.description: single item in each list, distinct values
same value in both lists merges weights
inputs given out of order must come back sorted by value
values present in only one list still appear

stress.description: two lists of distinct values with heavy overlap to stress the merge tally
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    vals = rng.sample(range(1, 1001), min(n, 1000))
    half = max(1, len(vals) // 2)
    pool1 = vals[:half + len(vals) // 4]
    pool2 = vals[half - len(vals) // 4:]
    items1 = [[v, rng.randint(1, 1000)] for v in pool1]
    items2 = [[v, rng.randint(1, 1000)] for v in pool2]
    rng.shuffle(items1)
    rng.shuffle(items2)
    if not items1:
        items1 = [[1, 1]]
    if not items2:
        items2 = [[1, 1]]
    return [items1, items2]
